name: ionic
description: The Ionic command-line interface (CLI) is the go-to tool for developing Ionic apps

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build web assets and prepare your app for any platform targets

options.name: --engine
options.description: Target engine (e.g. browser, cordova)

options.name: --platform
options.description: Target platform on chosen engine (e.g. ios, android)

options.name: --prod
options.description: Flag to use the production configuration

name: --configuration
-c
options.description: Specify the configuration to use

options.name: --source-map
options.description: Output source maps

options.name: --watch
options.description: Rebuild when files change

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: Enables tab-completion for Ionic CLI commands

subcommands.name: docs
subcommands.description: Open the Ionic documentation website

name: --browser
-w
options.description: Specifies the browser to use (safari, firefox, google chrome)

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Print project, system, and environment information

options.name: --json
options.description: Print system/environment info in JSON format

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize existing projects with Ionic

args.name: name
args.description: The name of your project (e.g. myApp, "My App")

options.name: --type
options.description: Type of project (e.g. angular, react, vue, custom)

name: --force
-f
options.description: Initialize even if a project already exists

options.name: --multi-app
options.description: Initialize a multi-app project

options.name: --project-id
options.description: Specify a slug for your app

options.name: --default
options.description: Mark the initialized app as the default project

subcommands.name: link
subcommands.description: Connect local apps to Ionic

args.name: id
args.description: The Appflow ID of the app to link (e.g. a1b2c3d4)

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Log in to Ionic

args.name: email
args.description: Your email address

args.name: password
args.description: Your password

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Log out of Ionic

subcommands.name: repair
subcommands.description: Remove and recreate dependencies and generated files

options.name: --cordova
options.description: Only perform the repair steps for Cordova platforms and plugins

subcommands.name: serve
subcommands.description: Start a local dev server for app dev/testing

options.name: --external
options.description: Host dev server on all network interfaces (i.e. --host=0.0.0.0)

options.name: --host
options.description: Use specific host for the dev server

name: --port
-p
options.description: Use specific port for the dev server

options.name: --public-host
options.description: The host used for the browser or web view

options.name: --no-livereload
options.description: Do not spin up dev server--just serve files

options.name: --lab-host
options.description: Use specific host for Ionic Lab server

options.name: --lab-port
options.description: Use specific port for Ionic Lab server

options.name: --no-open
options.description: Do not open a browser window

name: --browser
-w
options.description: Specifies the browser to use (safari, firefox, google chrome)

name: --browseroption
-o
options.description: Specifies a path to open to (/#/tab/dash)

name: --lab
-l
options.description: Test your apps on multiple platform types in the browser

options.name: --ssl
options.description: Use HTTPS for the dev server

options.name: --prod
options.description: Flag to use the production configuration

options.name: --consolelogs
options.description: Print app console logs to the terminal

options.name: --consolelogs-port
options.description: Use specific port for console logs server

name: --configuration
-c
options.description: Specify the configuration to use

options.name: --source-map
options.description: Output sourcemaps

subcommands.name: signup
subcommands.description: Create an Ionic account

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Create a new project

args.name: name
args.description: The name of your new project (e.g. myApp, "My App")

args.name: template
args.description: The starter template to use (e.g. blank, tabs; use --list to see all)

name: --list
-l
options.description: List available starter templates

options.name: --type
options.description: Type of project to start (e.g. vue, angular, react, ionic-angular, ionic1)

options.name: --cordova
options.description: Include Cordova integration

options.name: --capacitor
options.description: Include Capacitor integration

options.name: --no-deps
options.description: Do not install npm/yarn dependencies

options.name: --no-git
options.description: Do not initialize a git repo

options.name: --link
options.description: Connect your new app to Ionic

options.name: --id
options.description: Specify an Ionic App ID to link

options.name: --project-id
options.description: Specify a slug for your app (used for the directory name and package name)

options.name: --package-id
options.description: Specify the bundle ID/application ID for your app (reverse-DNS notation)

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Config commands for Ionic

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Print config values

args.name: property
args.description: The property name you wish to get

name: --global
-g
options.description: Use global CLI config

options.name: --json
options.description: Output config values in JSON

options.name: --root
options.description: Operate on root of ionic.config.json

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Set config values

args.name: property
args.description: The property name you wish to set

args.name: value
args.description: The new value of the given property

name: --global
-g
options.description: Use global CLI config

options.name: --json
options.description: Always interpret value as JSON

options.name: --force
options.description: Always overwrite existing values

options.name: --root
options.description: Operate on root of ./ionic.config.json

subcommands.name: unset
subcommands.description: Delete config values

args.name: property
args.description: The property name you wish to delete

name: --global
-g
options.description: Use global CLI config

options.name: --root
options.description: Operate on root of ./ionic.config.json

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: AppFlow deploy commands for Ionic

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Adds Appflow Deploy to the project

options.name: --app-id
options.description: Your Appflow app ID

options.name: --channel-name
options.description: The channel to check for updates from

options.name: --update-method
options.description: The update method that dictates the behavior of the plugin

options.name: --max-store
options.description: The maximum number of downloaded versions to store on the device

options.name: --min-background-duration
options.description: The minimum duration after which the app checks for an update in the background

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Create a deploy build on Appflow

options.name: --environment
options.description: The group of environment variables exposed to your build

options.name: --channel
options.description: The channel you want to auto deploy the build to. This can be repeated multiple times if multiple channels need to be specified

options.name: --commit
options.description: Commit (defaults to HEAD)

options.name: --skip-download
options.description: Skip downloading build artifacts after command succeeds

subcommands.name: configure
subcommands.description: Overrides Appflow Deploy configuration

args.name: platform
args.description: The native platform (e.g. ios, android)

options.name: --app-id
options.description: Your Appflow app ID

options.name: --channel-name
options.description: The channel to check for updates from

options.name: --update-method
options.description: The update method that dictates the behavior of the plugin

options.name: --max-store
options.description: The maximum number of downloaded versions to store on the device

options.name: --min-background-duration
options.description: The minimum duration after which the app checks for an update in the background

subcommands.name: manifest
subcommands.description: Generates a manifest file for the deploy service from a built app directory

subcommands.name: git
subcommands.description: Git commands for Ionic

subcommands.name: remote
subcommands.description: Adds/updates the Appflow git remote to your local Ionic app

subcommands.name: package
subcommands.description: Package commands for Ionic

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Create a package build on Appflow

args.name: platform
args.description: The platform to package (android, ios)

args.name: type
args.description: The build type (debug, release, development, ad-hoc, app-store, enterprise)

options.name: --signing-certificate
options.description: Signing certificate

options.name: --environment
options.description: The group of environment variables exposed to your build

options.name: --native-config
options.description: The group of native config variables exposed to your build

options.name: --destination
options.description: The configuration to deploy the build artifact to the app store

options.name: --commit
options.description: Commit (defaults to HEAD)

options.name: --build-stack
options.description: Target platform ("Android", "iOS - Xcode 11 (Preferred)", "iOS - Xcode 10")

options.name: --build-file-name
options.description: The name for the downloaded build file

options.name: --ipa-name
options.description: The name for the downloaded ipa file

options.name: --dsym-name
options.description: The name for the downloaded dsym file

options.name: --apk-name
options.description: The name for the downloaded apk file

options.name: --aab-name
options.description: The name for the downloaded aab file

options.name: --artifact-type
options.description: The artifact type (aab, apk, ipa, dsym)

options.name: --skip-download
options.description: Skip downloading build artifacts after command succeeds

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Deploys a binary to a destination, such as an app store using Appflow

args.name: build-id
args.description: The build id of the desired successful package build

args.name: destination
args.description: The destination to deploy the build artifact to the app store

subcommands.name: ssl
subcommands.description: SSL commands for Ionic

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: Generates an SSL key & certificate

options.name: --key-path
options.description: Destination of private key file

options.name: --cert-path
options.description: Destination of certificate file

options.name: --country-name
options.description: The country name (C) of the SSL certificate

options.name: --state-or-province-name
options.description: The state or province name (ST) of the SSL certificate

options.name: --locality-name
options.description: The locality name (L) of the SSL certificate

options.name: --organization-name
options.description: The organization name (O) of the SSL certificate

options.name: --common-name
options.description: The common name (CN) of the SSL certificate

name: --bits
-b
options.description: Number of bits in the key

subcommands.name: ssh
subcommands.description: SSH commands for Ionic

subcommands.description: Add an SSH public key to Ionic

args.name: pubkey-path
args.description: Location of public key file to add to Ionic

options.name: --use
options.description: Use the newly added key as your default SSH key for Ionic

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Delete an SSH public key from Ionic

args.name: key-id
args.description: The ID of the public key to delete

subcommands.description: Generates a private and public SSH key pair

args.name: key-path
args.description: Destination of private key file

name: --type
-t
options.description: The type of key to generate: ecdsa, ed25519, rsa

name: --bits
-b
options.description: Number of bits in the key

name: --annotation
-C
options.description: Annotation (comment) in public key. Your Ionic email address will be used

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: List your SSH public keys on Ionic

options.name: --json
options.description: Output SSH keys in JSON

subcommands.name: setup
subcommands.description: Setup your Ionic SSH keys automatically

subcommands.name: use
subcommands.description: Set your active Ionic SSH key

args.name: key-path
args.description: Location of private key file to use
